title: cornus alba f. gouchaultii
intro: Cornus alba f. gouchaultii, also known as the Red-veined Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the Cornaceae family, characterized by its bright red stems and dark green leaves with red veins. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in treating various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Cornus alba f. gouchaultii include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
